title: CrossMatrixModel
description: A specialized, configurable, SPARSE N-dimensional weighted cross-reference model (generalized House of Quality). OBSERVATION-ONLY (no LLM-authored numbers): the LLM emits only categorical observation tokens from a closed, declared vocabulary (+ evidence); numbers enter only via (a) a declared observation->value table (`scales`), (b) measurement from an authoritative source (provenanceClass=observed, carried as `measuredValue` + sourceRef, bucketed by CODE), or (c) engine computation. VALENCE BY SEPARATION (TRIZ): support and opposition are SEPARATE unipolar relations (relationType e.g. supports vs undermines), NOT a signed/bipolar scale — so a strong-for and a strong-against never cancel; the engine reports net AND tension. MULTI-OBSERVATION CELLS (poka-yoke): a Cell holds a SET of source-stamped observations (never a single collapsible value) so disagreement is preserved; the engine computes a consensus for math + a tension/conflict finding. N-DIMENSIONALITY is open (arbitrary `dimensions` + pairwise `relations` + `contractions`); adding a cross-dimension is additive. FMECA is a DIMENSION (criticality imported with a deterministic sourceRef). Operations FIXED (contract/marginalize/slice + driver queries); priority(AHP)/dense-tensors deferred; minimal sensitivity is NOT deferred (findings carry ranked `contributors`). Roofs + Hyperedges REMOVED (architecture-validity): intra-axis correlation = a self-relation (from==to); irreducible N-way + dense tensors are out of scope. Write API = a mutation-first design.

scales.description: Declared observation->value tables (the ONLY place tokens become numbers). Unipolar (values >= 0); valence is a separate relation, not a sign.

Level.description: One rung of a scale: the categorical observation token an LLM may emit, the (UNIPOLAR, >=0) number CODE assigns, and a rubric description. For a `measured` scale, `range` buckets a measured value to this level deterministically.

description.description: Semantic RUBRIC ANCHOR shown to the LLM (it chooses from token+description, never the value). Strongly recommended on every level.

value.description: Deterministic numeric the engine assigns. UNIPOLAR (>=0); opposition is a separate relation, not a negative value. LLM never sees/sets this.

range.description: For a `measured` scale: the numeric interval a measuredValue must fall in to bucket to this level (engine-applied).

mode.description: categorical = LLM picks a token; measured = a deterministic source value (measuredValue) is bucketed into a level by its `range` (LLM still authors no number).

levels.description: Unipolar observation->value table. Engine rejects unknown tokens.

DimensionKind.description: A TYPED DISPATCH discriminator: the engine binds deterministic kind-capabilities to it (weight-source policy, valid relations/scales, sibling-core routing, default profile/findings) via a DECLARED kind-capability profile. It affords/validates/routes; it must NOT import domain ALGORITHMS (those stay federated in the owning system).

RelationType.description: Includes BOTH supporting and OPPOSING valences as distinct types (separation, not a signed scale). `dependsOn` (typically a self-relation, from==to) carries a dependency matrix; its cyclic analysis (SCC/layering) routes to the graph core.

SourceRef.description: A VERIFIABLE pointer to the authoritative artifact (federation). The LLM can re-open `sourceRef`(+`locator`) to verify; `contentHash` (fast, non-crypto) lets the engine re-hash and detect staleness. `system` present => a deterministic import (a numeric weight/measuredValue is permitted).

system.description: the federated owner system, e.g. fmeca | telemetry | research

sourceRef.description: id/path the LLM can re-open (e.g. a file path).

locator.description: Sub-source location (line range, node id, selector).

contentHash.description: FAST NON-cryptographic identity+change hash at observation time (e.g. xxh3/fnv). Re-hash later -> staleness. NOT for security.

Evidence.description: A verifiable grounding the LLM can re-open and the engine can re-hash.

Observation.description: ONE source-stamped observation on a cell. The LLM emits the `observation` token; `measuredValue` is the measured-source path (observed provenance only, bucketed by code). Cells hold a SET of these so disagreement is preserved.

observation.description: Categorical token in the relation's scale (engine resolves to a value; rejects unknown tokens).

measuredValue.description: MEASURED PATH: a value from a deterministic source, permitted ONLY with provenanceClass=observed + a sourceRef. Engine buckets it to a token via the scale's level `range`s. LLM never sets this.

LifecycleStatus.description: Non-destructive lifecycle for auditable deprecation (append-only); deprecated elements are excluded from analyses.

weightObservation.description: LLM PATH: a categorical importance/criticality token, resolved by the dimension's weightScale. Weighted analyses (marginalize/findings) REQUIRE a resolvable member weight (validated as a precondition).

weight.description: DETERMINISTIC-IMPORT PATH ONLY: permitted iff sourceRef.system is set (e.g. fmeca criticality). LLM may not set this.

Cell.description: A NON-EMPTY tie holding a SET of source-stamped observations (poka-yoke: no single-value slot to collapse). Engine computes a consensus value for math + a tension/conflict finding when observations diverge.

Relation.description: A sparse pairwise relation between two dimensions, of one VALENCE (relationType — supporting OR opposing). Holds only non-empty cells. `from == to` expresses INTRA-axis correlation (a self-relation) — the former 'roof', now subsumed with no dedicated construct.

coveragePolicy.description: Turns coverage from a report into a gate: which side's members MUST have a cell, else an orphan is a violation.

Contraction.description: A declared multilinear cascade over a chain of relations (each `to` == next `from`; acyclic + scale-compatible, engine-validated). Contract a SINGLE valence at a time (support chain vs oppose chain), then compare — signs never mix.

Finding.description: READ-ONLY engine output. Derived findings carry `contributors` (ranked top drivers = the minimal sensitivity that makes 'what needs fixing' actionable) + a lineage `path` with resolvable sourceRefs.

contributors.description: Top contributing cells/members ranked by their share of the score (minimal sensitivity).

net.description: Engine-computed: support minus oppose (relative rank, not a probability).

tension.description: Engine-computed co-occurrence of support AND opposition (what bipolar net-zero would have hidden).
